description: In Xhosa, click consonants (c, x, q) can be either aspirated (ch, xh, qh) or nasalized (nc, nx, nq). The prenasalized aspirated clicks (nkc, nkx, nkq) involve compound modifications: prenasalization plus aspiration, distinguished by an added 'k' to avoid confusion with nasal clicks. This represents a complex set of allophonic and phonemic modifications.
sources: Wikipedia contributors. (2025). _Xhosa language_. Wikipedia, The Free Encyclopedia.
Gxilishe, S. (2002). _The Acquisition of Clicks by Xhosa-Speaking Children_. University of Cape Town.
DoBeS. (2008). _A Phonetic and Phonological Report on Xhosa_. University of Cologne.
